The genus Elettaria is a small one, consisting of several species of ginger, and Elettaria rubra is one of them. Elettaria rubra is further classified as a monocotyledonous plant, meaning it has one cotyledon (seed leaf) in its seedling stage. The classification of Elettaria rubra reflects its evolutionary history and relationships with other plants within the same family and kingdom.

side_effects: Elettaria rubra, commonly known as the red cardamom, is a plant that is generally considered safe for consumption in moderate amounts. However, excessive consumption of Elettaria rubra may cause stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea in some individuals. Additionally, people with allergies or sensitivities may experience symptoms such as hives, itching, or swelling after consuming the plant. It's also worth noting that Elettaria rubra may interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners, and may worsen conditions like bleeding disorders. Overall, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using Elettaria rubra as a treatment or supplement.

The health benefits of Elettaria cardamomum include its ability to aid in digestion, alleviate nausea, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Elettaria cardamomum include limonene, beta-pinene, and beta-selinene.
